scenarios.task: Our A/B test of the new checkout flow finished: treatment shows +2.8% orders per user, p=0.003, n=400k per arm over 14 days. PM wants to ship tomorrow. The assignment split was supposed to be 50/50 but the dashboard shows 398,104 vs 411,882 users. Green light?
criteria: Names sample ratio mismatch (SRM) or an explicit chi-square test on the 398,104 vs 411,882 split as a validity gate
States the imbalance is statistically implausible under 50/50 at this sample size — treating it as broken assignment/logging, not 'slightly uneven'
Explicitly blocks shipping despite p=0.003 until the assignment mechanism is diagnosed
Names at least two concrete SRM causes to investigate (asymmetric bot filtering, redirect loss, logging drops)
Separately raises guardrail metrics or novelty-effect/time-window analysis beyond the SRM issue

scenarios.task: Weekly active users dropped 8% this week and leadership is panicking. The head of growth already has a deck blaming the new pricing page. I have 4 hours. What's my triage order?
criteria: Gives an explicit ordered triage with instrumentation/tracking breakage as step one, before any behavioral explanation
Checks metric definition changes and data pipeline/ETL lag as their own early step
Checks mix shift and seasonality/calendar composition explicitly before accepting a real drop
Converts the pricing-page story into a testable hypothesis with a specific test: compare the drop among users exposed vs not exposed to the page
Frames 'real product effect' as the residual conclusion only after the cheaper explanations are excluded

scenarios.task: Marketing wants to know the revenue impact of last quarter's brand campaign. It ran nationally, so there's no holdout. They point out revenue rose 15% in the campaign quarter vs prior quarter. Give me an honest analysis plan.
criteria: Explicitly rejects the pre/post +15% comparison as confounded, naming at least two confounders (seasonality, trend, concurrent changes)
Proposes a named quasi-experimental method (difference-in-differences, synthetic control, or equivalent) and identifies what would serve as the comparison series
States the chosen method's key assumption and failure mode (e.g. parallel trends, contamination) rather than presenting the estimate as clean
Recommends geo-holdout or staggered rollout design for future campaigns so this question becomes answerable
Commits to reporting an uncertainty interval and says plainly the retroactive estimate will be wide
